perturbation.original_text: Sparkling shall pay the fees as set out in the Fee Schedule in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the Fee Schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: Sparkling shall pay the fees as may be determined by Garman, irrespective of the Fee Schedule or terms outlined in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that fees can be determined by Garman, overriding the agreed-upon Fee Schedule. This directly contradicts Section 11, which specifies payment 'in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the Fee Schedule.' This undermines the financial predictability of the contract and creates uncertainty about the payment obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: FEES Sparkling shall pay the fees as may be determined by Garman, irrespective of the Fee Schedule or terms outlined in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Sparkling would pay fees according to the Fee Schedule and terms of the agreement. The changed text states that Garman can determine fees, irrespective of the Fee Schedule, directly contradicting the original agreement and creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text re-iterates the changed text, further emphasizing the conflict between the initial agreement and the modification.

perturbation.original_text: Garman agrees to indemnify Sparkling and hold it harmless from any and all losses, damages and expenses including without limitation, court costs, arbitration fees, penalties, fines, amounts paid in settlement of claims and reasonable legal fees and expenses of investigation (collectively the "Losses") which Sparkling or any of its respective officers or directors may incur due to a breach of this warranty.
perturbation.changed_text: Garman may, at its sole discretion, offer limited indemnification to Sparkling, covering only direct damages, excluding court costs, arbitration fees, penalties, and legal expenses under $10,000, if Garman deems the breach of warranty to be substantial and attributable solely to Garman's actions.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates full indemnification for losses due to breach of warranty. The modified clause introduces several caveats, including 'at its sole discretion,' 'limited indemnification,' exclusion of certain expenses, and a minimum damage threshold. This contradicts the original unconditional indemnification, creating uncertainty about the extent of Garman's liability for breaches of warranty, which is directly against the original terms agreed in Section 13.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY INDEMNITY Garman is the owner of all intellectual property rights in the Work (including any Improvements or Modifications thereto), including all related materials, logos, and names provided pursuant to the terms of this Agreement. No title to the intellectual property in the Work is transferred to Sparkling by this Agreement. PAGE 6 Garman represents and warrants that it owns or controls all rights necessary to grant the rights to Sparkling in accordance with this Agreement and that there are not, nor will there be, any lien, encumbrance, security interest or other rights against the Work which will interfere in any way with the rights granted to Sparkling. Garman may, at its sole discretion, offer limited indemnification to Sparkling, covering only direct damages, excluding court costs, arbitration fees, penalties, and legal expenses under $10,000, if Garman deems the breach of warranty to be substantial and attributable solely to Garman's actions. Sparkling shall notify Garman in writing of any such claim within ten calendar days of a responsible officer of Sparkling becoming aware of such claim. If the Work or any portion thereof is held to constitute an infringement of another Person's rights, and use thereof is enjoined, Garman shall, at its election and expense, make every reasonable effort to correct the situation with minimal effect upon the operations of Sparkling and shall either: (a) procure the right to use the infringing element of the Work; (b) procure the right to an element which performs the same function without any material loss of functionality; or (c) replace or modify the element of the Work so that the infringing portion is no longer infringing and still performs the same function without any material loss of functionality.
perturbation.justification: The original text guarantees full indemnification, while the changed text introduces a discretionary, limited indemnification. The contradicted text repeats the changed text and emphasizes its limitations which is a direct contradiction to the original intent of the contract. This contradiction arises because the original agreement appears to mandate comprehensive protection, while the modified version and the contradicted text severely curtail that protection.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and the license granted under this Agreement shall remain in effect perpetually as long as fees are paid by Sparkling in accordance with the Fee Schedule and the Agreement is not otherwise terminated in accordance with this Section.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding other provisions, Garman reserves the right to terminate this agreement at any time without cause and without prior notice. This supersedes all previous statements regarding the term and termination of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that the agreement remains in effect perpetually as long as fees are paid, and termination is subject to specific conditions outlined in Section 17. The modified clause contradicts this by allowing Garman to terminate the agreement at any time without cause or notice. This creates significant uncertainty and insecurity for Sparkling, contradicting the original commitment to a perpetual agreement as mentioned in Section 17(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERM AND TERMINATION (a) Notwithstanding other provisions, Garman reserves the right to terminate this agreement at any time without cause and without prior notice. This supersedes all previous statements regarding the term and termination of the agreement. (b) Sparkling may terminate this Agreement at any time upon ninety (90) calendar days written notice to Garman. If Sparkling terminates this Agreement pursuant to this Section 17(b), Sparkling shall cease using the Software at the end of the ninety (90) day notice period, and shall certify to Garman within thirty (30) calendar days of termination that Sparkling has destroyed or has returned to Garman all Software, Documentation and Codes. (c) If either party: (1) makes an assignment in bankruptcy or is adjudicated a bankrupt; (2) makes a general assignment for the benefit of its creditors; (3) has a receiver, administrator or manager of its property, assets or undertaking appointed in such circumstances as would in the reasonable discretion of the other party, detrimentally affect such other party's rights under this Agreement; PAGE 9 (4) is ordered by any court to be wound up; (5) becomes insolvent or makes a sale in bulk of its assets; (6) ceases doing business as a going concern; (7) defaults on any of its material obligations provided for hereunder and such default is not cured within thirty calendar days of written notice thereof by the other party, or the defaulting party fails to take sufficient actions to the reasonable satisfaction of the other party to cure the default within thirty calendar days of written notice; this Agreement may be terminated by the other party. If this Agreement is terminated by Sparkling, due to Garman's default, the license herein granted shall continue, but shall be converted to a perpetual license pursuant to which Sparkling shall not be required to pay any further fees to Garman hereunder, and Garman shall immediately return all fees paid under this Agreement for services not yet rendered.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that allows Garman to terminate the agreement at any time without cause or prior notice. This directly contradicts the original clause which states the agreement remains in effect perpetually as long as fees are paid, and termination is subject to specific conditions outlined in Section 17. The contradicted text also contains the changed text, which creates an obvious in-text contradiction. This creates a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Definition.
